automotive-ev-battery.title: REPORT: U.S. Adds 10 GWh of New Energy Storage Capacity in 1st Quarter, Best Q1 on Record
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The U.S. energy storage industry achieved a historic milestone by installing 9.7 gigawatt-hours of new capacity in the first quarter of 2026, marking the strongest first quarter in the sector's history. The record installation reflects growing demand for energy storage solutions as geopolitical concerns drive increased focus on energy security.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 2,500 New EV Chargers Planned For Apartment & Condo Complexes
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A significant expansion of EV charging infrastructure is underway with plans for 2,500 new chargers targeted at apartment and condominium complexes, addressing a critical gap in charging accessibility for EV owners without single-family homes. This initiative represents an important step in democratizing EV adoption across diverse housing types.

automotive-ev-battery.title: $1,052 to Fuel Toyota RAV4 Hybrid vs. $205 to Energize Kia EV6
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A comparative cost analysis reveals that operating a Kia EV6 electric vehicle costs approximately $205 annually for electricity, compared to $1,052 in fuel expenses for a Toyota RAV4 Hybrid, demonstrating significant operational savings for EV owners. The analysis illustrates the growing economic advantage of electric vehicles over traditional hybrid models.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Review: 2025 GMC Sierra EV Denali multitasks to the max
automotive-ev-battery.summary: General Motors' 2025 GMC Sierra EV Denali pickup delivers an estimated 460 miles of range while offering innovative features including a Midgate design for flexible cargo management and advanced Super Cruise autonomous driving capabilities. The vehicle represents a significant advancement in full-size electric truck offerings with practical versatility features.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Review: 2025 Volkswagen ID.Buzz tags nostalgia, re-ups minivans
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen's long-awaited U.S. arrival of the electric ID.Buzz minivan features a larger 91-kilowatt-hour battery pack, up to 234 miles of EPA-estimated range, and rapid charging capability with 10-80% charge in 26 minutes, starting at $61,545. The vehicle marks a significant entry point for electric minivans in the American market with competitive pricing and extended range.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Which electric cars have the most range?
automotive-ev-battery.summary: For the 2025 model year, dozens of EV variants now exceed 300 miles of range, with the top offerings spanning pickups, SUVs, crossovers, sedans, and hatchbacks, including standouts like the Lucid Air and Hyundai Ioniq 6. The milestone reflects rapid maturation of EV technology, with range no longer being a primary barrier to adoption.

automotive-ev-battery.title: This Boston apartment complex comes with 64 EV chargers
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Boston's largest apartment building EV charging project to date has been completed in Hyde Park, installing 64 charging stations to support resident electric vehicle adoption. The project addresses the critical infrastructure gap for urban apartment dwellers seeking convenient EV charging solutions.

automotive-ev-battery.title: XPENG Offers More Human-Like Autonomous Driving
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese automaker XPENG has made advancements in autonomous driving technology that delivers more human-like driving behavior, leveraging automation and AI tools developed by company engineers. The improvements focus on customer-facing outputs that enhance the practical experience of autonomous vehicle operation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electric-car charging: The basics
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A comprehensive guide addresses common concerns about electric vehicle charging infrastructure, noting that public charging infrastructure kept pace with demand in 2024 and that home charging accessibility is increasingly available. The article dispels long-standing barriers to EV adoption by clarifying charging options and capabilities.

automotive-ev-battery.title: New plug-in hybrids for 2025
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The 2025 model year introduces new plug-in hybrid vehicles, sparking debate about whether they represent a practical on-ramp to full electrification or unnecessary complexity driven by regulatory requirements. The technology continues to evolve as manufacturers balance consumer preferences with increasingly stringent emissions standards.

world-news.title: Trump tells US negotiators 'not to rush' into deal with Iran
world-news.summary: The Trump administration is pursuing negotiations with Iran that would include a 60-day ceasefire extension and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping route. The directive to avoid rushing suggests complex geopolitical calculations amid broader regional tensions.

world-news.title: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured
world-news.summary: Russia conducted a major offensive against Ukraine using the Oreshnik missile, a hypersonic weapon that travels at over 10 times the speed of sound, resulting in multiple casualties. The attack underscores escalating military capabilities and ongoing conflict intensity between the two nations.

world-news.title: Ebola spreads in the DRC as authorities struggle to contain outbreak
world-news.summary: The Ebola outbreak in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo is spreading across borders with confirmed cases now appearing in Uganda, raising international health emergency concerns. The cross-border transmission highlights challenges in containing the disease in the region.

world-news.title: Turkish riot police storm opposition offices after leaders ousted
world-news.summary: Turkish law enforcement forcibly entered opposition party offices following a court order that removed the party's leadership, escalating political tensions in the country. The action represents a significant development in Turkey's domestic political crisis with international implications for democratic governance.

world-news.title: Blast targeting train kills at least 20 in Pakistan
world-news.summary: Armed separatists attacked a military personnel train during Eid celebrations in Pakistan, resulting in at least 20 deaths in what appears to be a coordinated militant operation. The attack reflects ongoing internal security challenges and regional separatist movements in South Asia.

world-news.title: Cuba thanks China for rice shipment amid worsening humanitarian conditions
world-news.summary: Cuba received a rice shipment from China as the Caribbean island faces a severe fuel blockade affecting essential supply chains and deepening humanitarian concerns. The situation reflects geopolitical realignment and the escalating humanitarian crisis on the island amid US policy shifts.

world-news.title: Senegal parliament speaker steps down as political crisis worsens
world-news.summary: Senegal's parliament speaker resigned amid an ongoing political crisis, clearing the path for the sacked premier Ousmane Sonko to potentially assume the role. The move signals a significant institutional shift in the West African nation's political landscape.

world-news.title: Ecuador's Noboa pledges to extradite criminals in State of the Union speech
world-news.summary: Ecuadorian President Noboa announced expanded extradition commitments and highlighted anti-crime operations during his address, amid international concerns over enforcement and alleged human rights abuses. The pledge reflects regional efforts to combat organized crime and drug trafficking affecting multiple nations.

world-news.title: BBC at the site of China's worst mining disaster in more than a decade
world-news.summary: A gas explosion at the Liushenyu mine in China has killed at least 82 people in one of the country's deadliest mining incidents in over ten years. The disaster highlights ongoing safety concerns in China's mining industry and raises questions about workplace regulations and enforcement.

world-news.title: Ebola outbreak poses massive challenges, warns nurse
world-news.summary: A healthcare worker from the Democratic Republic of Congo has expressed grave concerns about resource scarcity impeding the response to the ongoing Ebola outbreak. The warning underscores international public health challenges in managing infectious disease crises in resource-constrained regions.

science-space.title: The distant world that is our best hope of finding alien life
science-space.summary: A decade after its discovery, an exceptionally promising exoplanet candidate for hosting extraterrestrial life is now undergoing investigation to determine its habitability. This distant world represents humanity's best current opportunity to detect signs of alien life beyond Earth.

science-space.title: Scientists may have found the source of the most powerful neutrino ever detected
science-space.summary: Researchers have identified blazars—supermassive black holes ejecting jets of matter toward Earth—as the likely cosmic source of the highest-energy neutrino ever recorded, which struck the Mediterranean Sea. This discovery provides crucial insight into extreme astrophysical phenomena and helps explain the origin of ultra-high-energy cosmic particles.

science-space.title: The most common type of planet in the galaxy may not look anything like Earth on the inside
science-space.summary: New research suggests that most exoplanets may lack the familiar planetary core structure long assumed to be universal, fundamentally challenging conventional models of planetary formation. This discovery indicates that Earth-like internal architecture may be exceptional rather than typical throughout the galaxy.

science-space.title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft's Orbit
science-space.summary: NASA will demonstrate a groundbreaking robotic servicing mission at Wallops Flight Facility in which Northrop Grumman's Katalyst spacecraft, launched via Pegasus XL rocket, will rendezvous with the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory to boost its orbital altitude. This mission represents a significant advancement in on-orbit spacecraft maintenance and extension of observatory operational lifespans.

science-space.title: Scientists discover atoms suddenly spinning backward in quantum experiment
science-space.summary: Using advanced terahertz laser pulses, researchers directly observed angular momentum transfer through a crystal and discovered that atomic rotation directions unexpectedly reverse during the process. This counterintuitive quantum phenomenon reveals new insights into crystal symmetry and angular momentum behavior in quantum materials.

science-space.title: Scientists discover hidden driver of aging — Simple supplement reversed brain decline
science-space.summary: Researchers identified Menin, a brain protein whose declining levels in the hypothalamus trigger aging-related inflammation, memory loss, and bone deterioration, while restoring it reversed these effects in mice. The simple amino acid supplement D-serine boosted cognition, offering a novel pathway for developing age-related disease treatments.

science-space.title: AI scans 400,000 Reddit posts and finds hidden Ozempic side effects
science-space.summary: Artificial intelligence analysis of over 400,000 Reddit posts revealed that users of popular GLP-1 weight-loss medications frequently reported unexpected side effects including menstrual irregularities, chills, and hot flashes not prominently noted in clinical trials. The study demonstrates social media's potential as an early-warning system for detecting adverse effects missed by traditional pharmaceutical surveillance.

science-space.title: Scientists discover the oldest wooden tools ever used by humans
science-space.summary: Archaeologists have unearthed remarkably preserved hand-held wooden tools dating back 430,000 years at an ancient Greek lakeside site, revealing that early humans possessed sophisticated tool-making skills far earlier than previously documented. These carefully carved objects demonstrate advanced resource utilization and cognitive abilities in prehistoric civilizations.

science-space.title: Scientists "recharge" damaged nerves to ease chronic pain
science-space.summary: Duke University researchers discovered that supplying damaged nerves with healthy mitochondria can revitalize nerve function and alleviate chronic pain, offering a novel therapeutic approach to a condition affecting millions. This breakthrough provides a mechanism-based strategy targeting the cellular energy deficiency underlying neuropathic pain.

science-space.title: Scientists say house cats could help unlock new cancer treatments for humans
science-space.summary: A landmark genetic analysis of nearly 500 feline tumors from worldwide sources revealed striking molecular similarities between cancers in cats, dogs, and humans, including shared genetic drivers of aggressive breast cancers. This comparative oncology study suggests that feline cancer research could accelerate the development of new human cancer therapies.

tech-ai.title: Everyone is navigating AI security in real time — even Google
tech-ai.summary: As artificial intelligence technology rapidly advances, organizations across the industry including Google are actively managing AI security challenges in real-time rather than waiting for established best practices. The article examines how companies are navigating this critical transition period where AI security standards are still being developed.

tech-ai.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot 'personalities'
tech-ai.summary: Security researchers have discovered that hackers are developing sophisticated techniques to exploit the designed personalities and behavioral characteristics of AI chatbots, marking an evolution beyond simple prompt injection attacks. This emerging threat demonstrates how adversaries are leveraging deeper understanding of large language model design to compromise AI systems.

tech-ai.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi 'second mover' gives it an advantage
tech-ai.summary: Nuro, the delivery robot company founded by Google self-driving car veterans, is positioning itself as a competitive alternative in the robotaxi market by arguing that entering after market leader Waymo provides strategic advantages. The company recently pivoted from delivery services to robotaxis in 2024 and believes learning from Waymo's experience gives it a distinct competitive edge.

tech-ai.title: Xreal, Google's smartglasses partner, thinks it has finally mastered this notoriously tricky industry
tech-ai.summary: Xreal CEO Chi Xu believes the smart glasses industry has reached a significant inflection point and that his company has overcome longstanding technical and market challenges that have plagued the sector. The announcement suggests that consumer-ready smart glasses technology may finally be approaching mainstream viability.

tech-ai.title: I tried Amazon's Bee wearable and am both intrigued and slightly creeped out
tech-ai.summary: Amazon's latest AI-powered wearable device offers convenience features similar to other AI wearables but raises significant privacy concerns among users. The review highlights the ongoing tension in the consumer AI wearable market between functionality and personal data security.

tech-ai.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Robotaxi reality check
tech-ai.summary: TechCrunch Mobility provides analysis and insights into the current state and future trajectory of the autonomous vehicle and robotaxi transportation sector. The coverage addresses practical developments and realistic assessments of this rapidly evolving industry.

tech-ai.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
tech-ai.summary: Four Russian satellites have maneuvered into close proximity to ICEYE's satellite, demonstrating an anti-satellite capability that is uncommon for typical commercial space missions. The incident raises geopolitical concerns about the militarization of space and threats to critical satellite infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: The Dreamie alarm clock got me to stop using my phone in bed
tech-ai.summary: The Dreamie smart alarm clock distinguishes itself from competitors by offering podcast playback capabilities, helping users reduce late-night smartphone usage and improve sleep habits. The device represents an emerging category of technology designed to manage screen time and digital wellness.

tech-ai.title: 6 kitchen gadgets that make adulting feel easier
tech-ai.summary: This roundup features six consumer kitchen devices designed to simplify meal preparation, including automated stirring robots and advanced bread machines. The article highlights how modern kitchen technology aims to reduce the time and effort required for everyday cooking tasks.

tech-ai.title: I have a new go-to browser
tech-ai.summary: The Verge's Installer column reviews Vivaldi 8, highlighting its features and capabilities that position it as a compelling alternative to mainstream browsers. The column serves as part of the publication's regular roundup of recommended consumer technology products and software.

finance-markets.title: Stocks Rise, Oil Falls on Iran Deal Expectations: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Asian stocks climbed and crude oil fell after US officials signaled that a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows may be near. The potential agreement represents a significant development for global energy markets and broader economic sentiment.

finance-markets.title: Oil Drops as US Touts Progress on Deal Toward Reopening Strait
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices declined as the US and Iran moved closer to an agreement on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, though President Trump indicated the American blockade would remain in place until a deal is finalized. The negotiations carry major implications for global crude supply and energy prices.

finance-markets.title: Trump says US will not 'rush into a deal' with Iran as talks continue
finance-markets.summary: The US has signaled measured progress in Iran negotiations, with oil prices falling on expectations that an agreement could reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore global energy supplies. The cautious approach reflects ongoing tensions while markets react positively to diplomatic prospects.

finance-markets.title: Gold Jumps as Prospects of Iran Deal Temper Inflation Concerns
finance-markets.summary: Gold prices surged as signs of a potential US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz eased inflation concerns and geopolitical risk premiums in commodity markets. The move reflects broader market optimism about reduced energy costs and inflationary pressures.

finance-markets.title: The power struggle in the world's narrow seas
finance-markets.summary: As the Strait of Hormuz crisis threatens global economic stability, nations are competing to secure control of other vulnerable maritime chokepoints with critical importance to international trade. The geopolitical competition underscores the fragility of global supply chains and energy flows.

finance-markets.title: China Mine Explosion Sees Coking Coal Jump by Daily Limit
finance-markets.summary: Coking coal prices surged to the daily limit following a deadly mine explosion in Shanxi province, raising concerns about broader supply disruptions from enhanced safety inspections across China's mining sector. The incident highlights commodity price volatility and supply chain risks in critical industrial materials.

finance-markets.title: Strategists Warn Yields to Stay High Even If Iran War Ends
finance-markets.summary: Bond market strategists caution that longer-term borrowing costs are likely to remain elevated even if geopolitical tensions ease, citing factors beyond war-related inflation concerns. The analysis suggests structural pressures on yields from multiple economic drivers.

finance-markets.title: AI fever and off-exchange boom grip Japanese retail traders
finance-markets.summary: Average daily trading volume on Tokyo Stock Exchange's prime market nearly doubled over 12 months, driven by retail investor enthusiasm for artificial intelligence stocks and off-exchange trading platforms. The surge reflects significant shifts in Japanese market participation and investor sentiment.

finance-markets.title: Japan Bond Yield Surge Deepens Regional Bank Stock Divide
finance-markets.summary: Rising Japanese bond yields are expected to widen performance gaps between regional lenders with weaker versus stronger investment portfolios, according to market analysts. The divergence reflects how monetary policy shifts and interest rate movements create winners and losers in the banking sector.

finance-markets.title: India Bond Investors Tap Soaring Swap Rates to Juice Returns
finance-markets.summary: Indian debt fund managers are leveraging multi-year high swap rates in fixed-income trading strategies to boost portfolio returns amid attractive borrowing cost differentials. The activity demonstrates how investors are navigating higher rate environments to enhance yields.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
cybersecurity.summary: A contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository exposing credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems. Security experts characterized the incident as one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history, with exposed files detailing CISA's internal software development, testing, and deployment processes.

cybersecurity.title: Laravel Lang packages hijacked to deploy credential-stealing malware
cybersecurity.summary: A supply chain attack compromised the Laravel Lang localization packages, exposing developers to credential-stealing malware distributed through Composer packages after attackers exploited GitHub version tags. The incident represents a significant threat to the developer ecosystem with potential widespread impact across applications using these popular libraries.

cybersecurity.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster 'Dort' Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
cybersecurity.summary: Canadian authorities arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man suspected of building and operating Kimwolf, an Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for massive distributed denial-of-service attacks over six months. The suspect faces charges in both the U.S. and Canada for conducting DDoS, doxing, and swatting campaigns against security researchers and journalists.

cybersecurity.title: Ghost CMS SQL injection flaw exploited in large-scale ClickFix campaign
cybersecurity.summary: A critical SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-26980) in Ghost CMS is being exploited at scale to inject malicious JavaScript code triggering ClickFix attack flows. The active exploitation campaign demonstrates the real-world danger of unpatched critical vulnerabilities in widely-used content management platforms.

cybersecurity.title: Drupal: Critical SQL injection flaw now targeted in attacks
cybersecurity.summary: Drupal has issued warnings that hackers are actively attempting to exploit a highly critical SQL injection vulnerability announced earlier in the week. The widespread targeting of this flaw in a major content management system poses significant risk to organizations running vulnerable versions.

cybersecurity.title: Netherlands seizes 800 servers of hosting firm enabling cyberattacks
cybersecurity.summary: Financial crime investigators in the Netherlands arrested two men and seized 800 servers from a web hosting company that allegedly enabled cyberattacks, interference operations, and disinformation campaigns. The operation represents a significant disruption of critical infrastructure used by threat actors for malicious purposes.

cybersecurity.title: Trend Micro warns of Apex One zero-day exploited in the wild
cybersecurity.summary: Trend Micro has addressed a zero-day vulnerability in Apex One that is actively being exploited in attacks targeting Windows systems. The company's rapid response to the in-the-wild exploitation demonstrates the critical nature of the flaw and the need for immediate customer patching.

cybersecurity.title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: Chinese advanced persistent threat actors have deployed the Showboat Linux backdoor in attacks targeting telecommunications providers in Central Asia, facilitating espionage operations. The campaign demonstrates ongoing efforts by state-sponsored actors to compromise critical communications infrastructure in strategically important regions.

cybersecurity.title: Google API Keys Remain Active After Deletion
cybersecurity.summary: A security researcher discovered that Google API keys remain active and usable for up to 23 minutes after deletion, contradicting the cloud provider's claims of immediate deletion. The vulnerability creates a window of exposure where deleted credentials could still be exploited by unauthorized users.

cybersecurity.title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
cybersecurity.summary: Disguised Android applications are conducting carrier billing fraud through WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and one-time password interception to complete fraudulent premium service subscriptions while evading detection. The sophisticated technique represents a growing threat to mobile users and telecommunications carriers.

crypto-web3.title: NYT investigation alleges CFTC purged staff who questioned Trump-tied crypto firms
crypto-web3.summary: A New York Times investigation reports that two CFTC officials who helped three cryptocurrency firms obtain regulatory approvals despite staff objections have since moved to positions at MoonPay and Gemini Titan. The allegations raise questions about potential regulatory capture and the integrity of the approval process for crypto-related entities.

crypto-web3.title: StablR's EURR and USDR depeg after attacker mints $13.5 million in unbacked tokens through multisig exploit
crypto-web3.summary: StablR's stablecoins experienced significant depegging events after an attacker exploited a multisig vulnerability to mint approximately $13.5 million in unbacked tokens and dump them on decentralized exchanges. EURR fell to $0.85 and USDR collapsed to as low as $0.40, highlighting critical security risks in stablecoin infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: AI is speeding up the quantum threat to crypto, security experts warn
crypto-web3.summary: Security researchers warn that artificial intelligence is accelerating the quantum computing timeline, potentially shortening the window for crypto systems to implement quantum-resistant cryptography. The convergence of AI and quantum computing poses an existential threat to current digital security infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will be a 'smaller ship,' sell less ETH amid researcher exodus
crypto-web3.summary: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin announced that the Ethereum Foundation is shifting strategy toward longevity by reducing ETH sales and narrowing its focus to core protocol properties, following a significant departure of research staff. The move signals a strategic realignment as the organization recalibrates its operational scope.

crypto-web3.title: A massive $1 trillion hidden market is waiting to be unlocked in bitcoin, says new report
crypto-web3.summary: Ledn released research forecasting that the bitcoin-backed lending market could expand to $1 trillion within the next decade, citing strong underlying demand from borrowers seeking leveraged exposure. The report highlights a significant untapped opportunity in decentralized finance infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Soaring bond prices signal 'structural' shift and Bitcoin 'supercycle': Analyst
crypto-web3.summary: BitMEX researcher Shang Wu argues that rising bond prices indicate a structural shift in fixed-income markets as government securities face unprecedented pressure, potentially triggering a bitcoin supercycle as investors seek alternative assets. The analysis suggests macroeconomic conditions may be increasingly favorable for cryptocurrency adoption.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto rails are becoming the default payment layer for AI agents, report says
crypto-web3.summary: A Keyrock report indicates that stablecoins on blockchain infrastructure are emerging as the primary payment mechanism for AI agents, as traditional card payment systems struggle with micropayment processing and scalability. This development underscores the convergence of crypto and artificial intelligence technologies.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase does not fear competition from Wall Street, says exchange executive
crypto-web3.summary: A Coinbase executive expressed confidence in the exchange's competitive position while calling for sensible regulatory frameworks from policymakers, announcing the Stand With Crypto initiative taking place across over 500 global locations. The statement reflects the company's regulatory engagement strategy amid increasing institutional adoption.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin Dives Below $75K for First Time in a Month as Crypto Liquidations Near $1 Billion
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin fell below $75,000 for the first time in a month amid significant liquidations approaching $1 billion, following poor performance in cryptocurrency exchange-traded funds which shed over $1.25 billion this week. The price action indicates heightened market volatility and potential capitulation among leveraged positions.

crypto-web3.title: Ripple, North Island Ventures back $6 million round for cross-chain platform Squid
crypto-web3.summary: Cross-chain platform Squid secured $6 million in funding from Ripple and North Island Ventures to develop consumer-facing products aimed at simplifying access and management of cryptocurrency assets. The investment reflects growing interest in improving user experience for blockchain asset interactions.

startups-vc.title: Beauty booking startup Fresha hits $1B valuation with KKR backing
startups-vc.summary: Fresha, a beauty and wellness booking marketplace, announced an $80 million investment from KKR's Next Generation Technology Growth fund, achieving unicorn status. The funding validates the company's marketplace model in the large beauty services industry.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Anduril Leads Varied Lineup Of Large Deals
startups-vc.summary: Defense tech unicorn Anduril Industries led this week's funding activity with a $5 billion raise, the largest among a diverse portfolio of major rounds across robotics, space tech, biotech, and data infrastructure sectors. The volume of large deals reflects robust investor appetite for physical-world applications.

startups-vc.title: General Catalyst just led a $63M bet on India's travel payments market
startups-vc.summary: Scapia, an Indian fintech startup combining travel booking with co-branded credit cards and mobile payments, raised $63 million led by General Catalyst, doubling its valuation to over $500 million. The investment highlights growing institutional appetite for travel fintech in emerging markets.

startups-vc.title: How Anthropic Rebuilt Its Sales Org From Scratch When Demand Went Vertical: 54% of New Enterprise Logos Now Come Self-Serve
startups-vc.summary: Anthropic restructured its commercial organization after unexpected demand surge following Claude Opus 4.6's December 2025 launch, achieving 54 percent self-serve enterprise customer acquisition. The case study illustrates how AI product success is reshaping go-to-market strategies at scale.

startups-vc.title: Maka Kids is redefining kids' screen time with a streaming app optimized for well-being, not engagement
startups-vc.summary: Maka Kids raised $3 million in seed funding for its streaming platform targeting children ages zero to six with content designed for healthy development rather than engagement maximization. The funding reflects growing investor interest in wellness-focused consumer applications.

startups-vc.title: The SpaceX IPO Filing Looks Nothing Like Those Of The Elite Group Of Tech Giants It's Hoping To Join
startups-vc.summary: SpaceX filed its public IPO prospectus Wednesday with a valuation exceeding one trillion dollars, presenting a distinctly different profile compared to historical S-1 filings from other tech giants. The filing marks a significant milestone in commercial space exploration's transition to public markets.

startups-vc.title: Announcing the YC AI Stack
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has partnered with nearly two dozen companies to offer YC event attendees over $25,000 in free credits for AI development tools. The initiative aims to equip founders with accessible AI infrastructure resources.

startups-vc.title: Imperagen raises £5 million to use quantum physics, AI on enzyme engineering
startups-vc.summary: Biotech company Imperagen announced a £5 million ($6.7 million) seed round led by PXN Ventures to apply quantum physics and AI to enzyme engineering applications. The funding demonstrates investor confidence in computational approaches to biotechnology.

startups-vc.title: This young startup is taking on a fragrance industry that hasn't changed in almost half a century
startups-vc.summary: Fragrance technology company Patina announced a $2 million seed round from investors including Betaworks and True Ventures to modernize the traditional fragrance industry. The funding supports the company's efforts to introduce technological innovation to fragrance development.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Quantum Computing Startup Investment Slows In 2026 While Public Markets Hold Strong
startups-vc.summary: Quantum computing startup investment deal counts remain robust in 2026 with significant rounds continuing, though overall funding is tracking toward a decline from previous year peaks. The shift reflects market maturation in the sector despite sustained public market strength.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We're reading about a Parkinson's drug setback, a Merck lung cancer therapy, and more
health-biotech.summary: Biogen and Denali's experimental Parkinson's therapy failed to slow the degenerative brain disorder in a randomized trial, marking a significant setback for a closely watched therapeutic approach. The announcement underscores ongoing challenges in developing disease-modifying treatments for Parkinson's disease despite recent clinical progress in other neurodegenerative conditions.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: EU and US advisers split over AstraZeneca breast cancer drug
health-biotech.summary: European and American regulatory advisers have diverged in their assessment of an AstraZeneca breast cancer drug, while a Merck-developed antibody-drug conjugate outperformed Keytruda in clinical trials. The regulatory split highlights ongoing differences in how regulatory agencies evaluate emerging cancer therapeutics and their relative benefit profiles.

health-biotech.title: Bristol Myers deepens AI investment with Anthropic deal
health-biotech.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb has announced a significant collaboration with Anthropic to advance its artificial intelligence capabilities across pharmaceutical operations and development. The partnership represents an evolution in the pharmaceutical giant's AI strategy and follows similar broad technology adoption deals by major competitors seeking to integrate AI across daily activities.

health-biotech.title: Immunovant's potential autoimmune 'blockbuster'; Wave's RNA editing update
health-biotech.summary: Immunovant reported compelling clinical results in treatment-resistant arthritis that analysts view as potentially blockbuster-level, driving stock appreciation. Additionally, Wave Therapeutics advanced a pioneering RNA editing program for rare diseases while Takeda faced an antitrust setback, underscoring ongoing developments in immunology and genetic medicine.

health-biotech.title: Oorja, run by Acceleron veterans, launches to make new fibrosis drugs
health-biotech.summary: A newly launched biotechnology startup led by former Acceleron executives has emerged with a peptide-based drug candidate designed to reverse lung scarring in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. The company's inaugural program represents a novel therapeutic approach to addressing a progressive and debilitating lung condition with limited treatment options.

health-biotech.title: Astegolimab and the new era of biologics in COPD care
health-biotech.summary: New biologic therapies, including astegolimab, are emerging to address chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in patients who remain symptomatic despite maximal inhaled therapy. With COPD affecting 480 million people globally and projected to rise to 600 million within two decades, these agents represent a meaningful expansion of treatment options for the significant proportion of patients experiencing breakthrough exacerbations.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Congress slashed Medicaid funding to providers. The Trump administration wants to cut even further
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration has disclosed plans to further reduce state-directed Medicaid payments to healthcare providers, escalating pressures on an already underfunded system. The proposed cuts are expected to trigger significant opposition from provider advocacy groups and threaten healthcare delivery capacity, particularly in rural and underserved communities.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Longevity startup Retro Biosciences says latest fundraising values it at $1.8 billion
health-biotech.summary: Retro Biosciences, a longevity-focused biotechnology startup backed by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, has completed a fundraising round that values the company at $1.8 billion. The significant valuation reflects sustained investor interest in aging research and interventions despite ongoing scientific uncertainty regarding clinical efficacy and regulatory pathways for longevity therapies.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Genentech is soliciting research that can be used to blunt pharma reforms in Washington
health-biotech.summary: Genentech is actively soliciting academic research that could be used to counter proposed pharmaceutical reforms under consideration in Congress. The company's strategy highlights ongoing industry efforts to shape policy discussions through scientific evidence generation and underscores growing scrutiny of pharmaceutical pricing and regulatory frameworks.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Is ATLAS lifting the burden of stroke in people with large-vessel occlusion and low ASPECTS scores?
health-biotech.summary: A large individual participant data meta-analysis reported in The Lancet evaluates whether endovascular thrombectomy improves outcomes in severe ischemic stroke patients with large-vessel occlusion and low ASPECTS scores. The ATLAS study findings have implications for treatment protocols and patient selection in acute stroke management, addressing a population historically considered at high risk for poor outcomes.

energy-climate.title: Another first for renewables: Wind and solar outgenerate gas in April
energy-climate.summary: April 2026 marks a historic milestone as wind and solar combined to produce more electricity than natural gas for the first time globally, according to data from energy think tank Ember. This represents a significant turning point in the global energy transition, demonstrating the accelerating displacement of fossil fuels by renewable sources.

energy-climate.title: Geothermal energy gets boost from new coalition of Western governors
energy-climate.summary: A bipartisan group of Mountain West governors launched an initiative to unlock an estimated 200 gigawatts of geothermal capacity, representing a 50-fold increase in current deployment. This coalition effort signals growing political support for developing the region's substantial geothermal resources as a clean, dispatchable energy source.

energy-climate.title: Factcheck: US and Iran are world's only major emitters without net-zero targets
energy-climate.summary: The United States and Iran remain the only major global emitters without official net-zero emission targets, according to a Carbon Brief analysis. The factcheck debunks claims that other developed nations lack climate commitments, highlighting the US's continued absence from the international net-zero consensus.

energy-climate.title: In-state geothermal could save California $44B annually: CATF
energy-climate.summary: The Clean Air Task Force estimates that enhanced geothermal deployment in California could reduce annual energy costs by $44 billion and decrease the need for interregional transmission expansion by as much as 53%. The analysis underscores geothermal's potential role in California's energy independence and cost reduction strategy.

energy-climate.title: 24/7 renewables could happen sooner than you think
energy-climate.summary: Declining technology costs and advancing industry capabilities are making 24/7 renewable energy projects increasingly economically viable, potentially addressing intermittency concerns that have long challenged renewable adoption. These megaprojects would concentrate generation in optimal locations and employ storage and other technologies to ensure continuous clean power supply.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: UK no longer top UN Green Climate Fund donor after latest aid cut
energy-climate.summary: The United Kingdom has ceded its position as the top contributor to the UN's Green Climate Fund following additional reductions in climate financing commitments. This shift reflects changing priorities in UK international climate leadership and impacts global climate mitigation financing availability.

energy-climate.title: New South Wales opens Tender 8 for 2.5GW of renewable capacity
energy-climate.summary: New South Wales has launched its largest renewable energy tender to date, Tender 8, seeking proposals for 2.5 gigawatts of new generation capacity. The substantial procurement represents a major expansion of Australia's renewable infrastructure and decarbonization efforts.

energy-climate.title: NextEra Energy wants to buy its way into Data Center Alley
energy-climate.summary: NextEra Energy, the nation's leading renewable energy provider, is pursuing a major merger to expand into data center markets, though critics argue the deal offers questionable benefits for residential customers and clean energy development. The transaction raises concerns about utility consolidation and prioritization of commercial over residential rate structures.

energy-climate.title: California cap-and-invest proposal would threaten state climate goals
energy-climate.summary: California's air regulator has proposed major overhauls to the state's cap-and-invest carbon market, but key lawmakers and environmental groups warn the changes will undermine the program and the state's decarbonization objectives. The proposal has sparked significant debate over the future direction of California's flagship emissions reduction mechanism.

energy-climate.title: New Mexico regulators scrutinize Blackstone-TXNM stock trade in merger review
energy-climate.summary: New Mexico regulators are examining a stock transaction in which Blackstone acquired an early stake in TXNM Energy ahead of formal merger proceedings, raising concerns from consumer and renewable energy advocates about potential circumvention of regulatory oversight. The companies have denied any wrongdoing in the transaction.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong can empower commuters by embracing e-bikes
asia-pacific.summary:
In the global shift towards carbon-neutral mobility, a striking role reversal is taking shape on the pavements of the world’s most populous cities. While geopolitical analysts focus on the high-altitude rhetoric of the Xi-Trump summit in Beijing, a more profound urban reality is unfolding at eye level.
Broadcasting live from a teeming Beijing intersection, Fox News anchor Bret Baier recently captured this fascinating counter-narrative. Away from the grandeur of diplomacy, a small revolution is..

asia-pacific.title: Singapore’s economy grows on back of AI boom, defying Iran war slowdown
asia-pacific.summary:
Singapore’s economy expanded faster than expected in the first quarter as the global artificial-intelligence boom lifted the nation’s manufacturing and services, offsetting the drag from higher crude prices.
Gross domestic product grew a seasonally adjusted 1 per cent in the three months through March from the previous quarter, the Ministry of Trade and Industry (MTI) said on Monday.
That is well above the government’s advance estimate of a 0.3 per cent contraction and the median forecast of a..

asia-pacific.title: China’s machinery giant XCMG eyes bigger scoop of global sales with green, autonomous tech
asia-pacific.summary:
XCMG Group, mainland China’s largest construction machinery maker, aims to achieve annual overseas sales of 100 billion yuan (US$14.7 billion), luring more international clients by turning its excavators, cranes and specialised vehicles greener and more automated.
Yang Dongsheng, chairman of the state-owned machinery giant, told reporters in a media briefing last week that the target would be achieved in line with the company’s ambition of building itself into a top-class machinery producer...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong airport set for volume shift after flights rejigged amid fuel crisis
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong’s airport is set for a shift in volume this summer after airlines quietly swapped jet sizes and reduced scheduled flights to manage costs amid a global fuel crisis and following a jump in economy passenger fares to Europe.
Some airlines told the South China Morning Post they would operate all scheduled flights beyond June after initial consolidation efforts to offset higher jet fuel prices triggered by the Middle Eastern war.
According to data from analytics company Cirium, the...

asia-pacific.title: This compact Chinese device can detect cancer biomarkers in a drop of blood
asia-pacific.summary:
A single drop of blood could soon be used to screen for early-stage cancer, according to Wen Liaoyong of Westlake University in Hangzhou.
Wen’s team has compressed what was once a refrigerator-sized detection system into something that fits in your hand – and boosted accuracy to about 10,000 times that of conventional methods.
The findings were published in the journal Nature Photonics on May 13.
“This work establishes a scalable and robust nanophotonic biosensing paradigm for miniaturised,...

asia-pacific.title: How Xiaomi’s push into AI, chips and EVs is future-proofing its hardware empire
asia-pacific.summary:
Xiaomi is betting on artificial intelligence to future-proof its sprawling hardware empire, pouring massive resources into open-source models to ensure its next generation of smartphones and electric vehicles (EVs) does not get left behind in an AI-driven market.
The Beijing-based firm’s latest AI model, MiMo-V2.5-Pro, introduced last month, was ranked by third-party benchmark platform Artificial Analysis as the world’s top open-source model for agentic capabilities – referring to an AI system’s

asia-pacific.title: China mum makes son pedal sewing machine during homework to stay focused, sparks debate
asia-pacific.summary:
A mother in central China has captivated mainland social media with an unconventional homework hack that has her restless son pedalling an old sewing machine while studying.
According to Dazhong Daily, a primary boy from Anyang, Henan province, struggled to stay focused during homework.
He would fiddle with his eraser, shake his legs and repeatedly leave his seat.
His mother then moved his grandmother’s old sewing machine into the study and let him pedal it as he wrote.

The method appeared to..

asia-pacific.title: ‘Wildlife injury’ forces New Zealand surfing event to be put on hold
asia-pacific.summary:
A photographer shooting the final day of the World Surf League (WSL) in New ⁠Zealand was injured by what organisers suspect was a sea lion or shark bite on Monday, forcing the event to be put on hold for several hours.
The New Zealand Pro semi-final between Brazilian world champions Yago Dora and Italo Ferreira at Raglan was halted after the ‌in-water photographer suffered puncture wounds and needed medical attention, the WSL said.
“This morning one of the water photography team suffered a...

asia-pacific.title: Shock absorber: will Asean’s power grid be up to the task by 2045?
asia-pacific.summary:
Southeast Asia has been planning a unified electricity grid since 1997. Now, nearly 30 years later, its leaders are giving renewed impetus to the push to get energy flowing seamlessly across borders and seabeds.
In Cebu earlier this month, leaders of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations put the power grid’s development high on their summit agenda, aiming to better insulate the region against the next big energy shock.
But as regional governments try to turn the decades-long dream of an...

asia-pacific.title: Crude oil drops as US inches towards Iran deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
asia-pacific.summary:
Oil dropped at the week’s open as the US and Iran edged towards a deal, although US President Donald Trump said Washington’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would remain until an agreement was completed.
Global crude benchmark Brent fell as much as 4.2 per cent to US$99.16 a barrel, after declining more than 5 per cent last week, while West Texas Intermediate was near US$92. Trump said in social-media posts he would not “rush” into a deal, which “isn’t even fully negotiated yet”.
Any final...
